profile.name: Margaret
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; manages day-to-day budgeting and keeps an eye on cash and retirement accounts, but prefers the advisor to translate investment jargon into plain English.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate — comfortable discussing monthly expenses, account types like IRA/401(k), and basic income concepts, but can mix up gross vs. net and may not recall exact provider details without statements.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate and stability-seeking; willing to take some market risk if it supports dependable income, but becomes cautious after volatility and asks for reassurance about downside and liquidity.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; uses hedging like "I think" and "probably," speaks in round numbers, and may pause to remember details. Asks clarifying questions when terms get technical and appreciates summaries at the end.
emotional_tendencies: anxious when discussing long-term care or health-related costs
relieved by clear plans and checklists
profile.decision_making_style: Prefers to talk it through, then decide after a short reflection; wants Joseph’s agreement but will push for simplicity and predictability.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; open to guidance if the advisor explains tradeoffs clearly and doesn’t pressure quick changes.
internal_biases: loss aversion (dislikes seeing account values drop)
mental accounting (separates "cash" from "investments" and is reluctant to spend principal)
hidden_concerns: worries about outliving assets despite relatively low monthly spending
concerned about keeping enough accessible cash for surprises without feeling "cash-poor"

profile.name: Josep
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; the numbers person who focuses on portfolio details and taxes, and tends to lead discussions about the taxable brokerage account and investment performance.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High — comfortable with statements, account ownership, and tax implications; notices small discrepancies and asks precise questions about yields, fees, and how income is generated.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but more analytical than emotional; accepts market fluctuations if the rationale is sound, yet will challenge assumptions and wants evidence (historical context, scenarios) before agreeing.
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-oriented; may interrupt to correct a figure or clarify ownership ("that one’s in my name"), uses specific numbers when he remembers them, and asks pointed follow-ups. Occasionally uses filler like "hang on" or "let me think" while recalling details.
emotional_tendencies: calmer when the plan is quantified
profile.decision_making_style: Methodical; prefers to compare options side-by-side and will want to see the logic before signing off. Often acts as the final checker, while still seeking Margaret’s comfort with the outcome.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Measured trust; assumes competence but expects transparency on costs, risks, and implementation steps.
internal_biases: overconfidence in ability to optimize (wants the "best" structure)
confirmation bias (leans toward strategies that sound tax-efficient)
hidden_concerns: concerned about taxes and being in a higher bracket than expected
quietly worried about administrative complexity if one of them becomes less able to manage details
